name: create-calendar-event
description: Create events in Google Calendar using OAuth2. Supports setting title, time range, timezone, and calendar selection.

OAUTH_TOKEN_SECRET.description: Secret path for the Google Calendar OAuth2 token (e.g. 'google/calendar_oauth_token')

CALENDAR_ID.description: Google Calendar ID to create the event in (use 'primary' for the main calendar)

START_TIME.description: Event start time in RFC3339 format (e.g. '2026-03-17T09:00:00-07:00')

END_TIME.description: Event end time in RFC3339 format (e.g. '2026-03-17T10:00:00-07:00')

TIMEZONE.description: IANA timezone name (e.g. 'America/New_York'). Defaults to UTC.

setup_instructions.title: Enable Google Calendar API
setup_instructions.detail: In Google Cloud Console, go to 'APIs & Services > Library' and enable the 'Google Calendar API'.

setup_instructions.title: Create OAuth credentials
setup_instructions.detail: In 'APIs & Services > Credentials', create an OAuth 2.0 Client ID. Add the 'https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar.events' scope.

setup_instructions.title: Obtain OAuth tokens
setup_instructions.detail: Run the OAuth authorization flow to grant calendar access. Capture the refresh token from the response.
